senses.definition: plural of pre modification; likely refers to modifications done beforehand in a process, system, or document.
examples: The project included several premodifications to the specification before production.
They discussed premodifications to the contract during the planning phase.

senses.definition: modified in advance; having changes applied before a main stage or event.
examples: The premodified data was used to speed up the analysis.
Premodified templates were ready for the workflow.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of premodify; to modify something beforehand.
examples: The system premodifies inputs to improve processing speed.
She premodifies the document to meet initial requirements.

senses.definition: to modify something beforehand.
examples: We plan to premodify the dataset before running the analysis.
Premodify the form to reduce errors.

senses.definition: the act of modifying something in advance.
examples: Premodifying the plan helped prevent later changes.
They are premodyifying the rules to suit the new policy.

senses.definition: to moisten something beforehand, often to prepare it for processing or use.
examples: They premoisten the soil to help seeds germinate.
Premoisten the cleaning cloth before scrubbing.

senses.definition: moistened beforehand.
examples: The premoistened towel was ready for use.
Use the premoistened wipes for easier cleaning.

senses.definition: the act of moistened beforehand; preparing by adding moisture in advance.
examples: Premoistening the fabric helps it absorb dye more evenly.
They are premistening the wound dressing before application.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of premoisten; moisten beforehand.
examples: The prep kit premistens the wipes for easier application.
It premistens the fabric so the dye spreads smoothly.

senses.definition: one of the teeth located between the canine and molars used for grinding food.
examples: People usually have four premolars in each jaw.
The dentist checked your premolars for cavities.

senses.definition: the plural of premolar; multiple teeth between canines and molars.
examples: Humans typically have eight premolars.
The chart shows the premolars labeled in blue.

senses.definition: to form or shape in advance; to cast or mold beforehand.
examples: They premold the components to fit the final design.
Premold bricks were used in the construction process.

senses.definition: already formed or shaped in advance.
examples: Premolded parts save time during assembly.
The premolded container fits perfectly.

senses.definition: a tool or device used to shape material in advance; also a person who preforms molding.
examples: The premolder was set up to create consistent shapes.
A skilled premolder prepared the ceramic pieces.

senses.definition: the act or process of shaping in advance; a preformed molding.
examples: Premolding stages are completed before final assembly.
The house has decorative premolding around doors.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of premold; to shape in advance.
examples: The factory premolds components for speed.
They premolds the ceramic pieces for uniformity.

senses.definition: the period before a molt, when an animal prepares to shed skin or exoskeleton.
examples: She observed premolt behavior in the crab.
During premolt, the insect becomes more active.

senses.definition: relating to a period before a monarchy or before a king rules; early or pre-monarchical.
examples: Premonarchal records describe early governance.
The premonearchal era is not well documented.

senses.definition: relating to the period before a monarchy; premonarchical.
examples: Premonarchial society faced different challenges.
Premonarchial laws were informal and local.

senses.definition: existing before a monarch; of or relating to a time before a king or queen.
examples: Premonarchical traditions influenced local governance.
The premonearchical era left few written records.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring before money is used; not yet monetary.
examples: Premonetary incentives were discussed before introducing currency.
The premontary stage focused on barter and value units.

senses.definition: alternative spelling or rare variant of premn monetary; not commonly used.
examples: Some texts reference premontory measures in early trade.
Premonetory concepts appear in ancient accounting.

senses.definition: relating to the period before Mongolian prominence or influence; pre-Mongolian.
examples: Premongolian cultures predate the Mongol Empire.
Premongolian art influences can be seen in nearby regions.

senses.definition: to warn or inform in advance; to notify beforehand.
examples: Let me premonish you about the risks involved.
The guide premonished the travelers of potential delays.

senses.definition: past tense of premonish; warned in advance.
examples: He premonished them about the storm.
They were premonished of the upcoming changes.
